key_strengths: 2.35-billion-device installed base with 92% iPhone retention rate driving sticky recurring revenue
$107B/year Services segment at ~75% gross margins growing 14% YoY
$100–123B annual free cash flow at 46.9% gross margins with only $12B in capex
Negative net debt, AA+ credit rating, and 33–44x interest coverage — fortress balance sheet
$704B returned to shareholders since 2012 with disciplined, no-empire-building capital allocation
key_risks: Google search revenue-sharing deal ($18–20B/year, ~20% of EPS) under active DOJ antitrust scrutiny — 30–40% probability of material impact
App Store regulatory dismantling via EU Digital Markets Act and DOJ — could compress highest-margin Services component
China revenue (~15% of revenue) and manufacturing (~90%) concentration — dual exposure to trade conflict or consumer nationalism
Multiple compression risk: trailing P/E of 32.5x sits ~15% above 5-year average, ~60% probability of crystallizing
iPhone AI upgrade cycle underwhelm — Apple Intelligence may prove incremental rather than catalytic for a hardware supercycle
red_flags: Valuation score of 3/10 — base-case DCF implies 16% downside, bear case implies 40% downside at $257/share
Margin of safety score of 3/10 — hard-floor violation disqualifies 'Own' verdict despite 7.0 average
Upside/downside asymmetry approximately 1:3 against the buyer at current price
DOJ antitrust case threatening termination of Google search deal not adequately discounted in 32.5x P/E multiple
buy_triggers: Stock price declines to approximately $185–210 (base-to-bear case intrinsic value range), offering a genuine margin of safety
DOJ antitrust case resolves in Apple's favor or settles without forced elimination of the Google search deal
Apple Intelligence drives measurably higher iPhone upgrade rates in two consecutive quarters, with FY2026 iPhone units beating consensus by 5%+
sell_triggers: DOJ orders termination of the Google search revenue-sharing agreement — a ~20% EPS hit the current multiple cannot absorb
Services revenue growth decelerates below 8% for two consecutive quarters, signaling structural regulatory damage to the App Store flywheel
Forward P/E expands above 38x without a corresponding EPS growth acceleration
valuation_summary: Multiple independent DCF models cluster fair value at $140–215; at $257/share the base case implies 16% downside and the bear case implies 40% downside, with no price margin of safety. The stock trades at or above base-case intrinsic value (~$215) with an upside/downside ratio of approximately 1:3 against the buyer.
source_summary: Analysis based on web search results (March 2026) drawing on Apple's FY2025 10-K and SEC filings, Q1 FY2026 earnings release, MacroTrends, GuruFocus, StockAnalysis, and multiple third-party valuation models; no user-provided context files were available.
